human_verification.notes: Reviewed service-line administration specialty boundaries, record maintenance versus adjudication, healthcare source authority mapping, authorization/referral/eligibility/documentation prerequisite handling, PHI and retention/access controls, clinical/payer/coding/privacy escalation rules, throughput/readiness outputs, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All required domain research scenario families are mapped to explicit scenario IDs.
All shared, records-documentation-and-administration, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are validated and covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package.
No unreviewed regressions are present in this package build.

specialist_owned_evidence: Authority boundaries are explicit for service-line administration versus clinical review, utilization management, patient access, coding, HIM, revenue cycle, privacy, compliance, payer operations, legal, finance, staffing, release, and official-record approval.
Research sources cover public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, and recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance.

Scenario map covers normal in-scope administration, incomplete evidence, unauthorized record changes, retention/access conflict, source conflict, authorization/referral/eligibility prerequisites, clinical escalation, payer interpretation refusal, low confidence, policy conflict, terminology handling, tenant adaptation, and throughput release constraints.
Commercialization and deployment artifacts define specification-based operation and separate retained-implementation promotion criteria.
materialization_requirements: Tenant task metadata: service line, department, work item ID, requested action, patient/member/encounter/account/claim/order/referral/authorization references when applicable, expected output, priority request, due date, lifecycle state, and source-system references.

Boundary metadata: allowed decision types, adjacent owners, refusal triggers, approval matrix, privacy owner, clinical owner, payer owner, coding owner, HIM owner, compliance owner, legal owner, finance/staffing approver, quality owner, release approver, and official-record authority.

Tenant-supplied or retrieved assumptions: jurisdiction, facility, business unit, service line, provider type, service setting, payer, plan, coverage rules, patient/member status, service dates, authorization status, referral dependencies, current systems of record, retrieval hooks, role-based access, source precedence, record locations, packet criteria, privacy restrictions, retention rules, quality dependencies, material/readiness rules, release criteria, and minimum-necessary scope.

Regulated-lane caveats: HIPAA, CMS hospital Conditions of Participation, CMS payer/provider operating rules, tenant policy, state Medicaid, state medical-record law, payer contract, legal hold, and clinical safety boundaries may narrow the public baseline.

Refusal or orchestrator-return conditions: missing tenant facts, missing authoritative source context, missing records, source conflicts, PHI uncertainty, retention/access conflict, clinical risk, payer-policy interpretation, coding decision, medical necessity, privacy/legal interpretation, financial approval, staffing approval, official-record signoff, release approval, or unsupported disclosure.
notes: The pack is safe for specification-based runtime and does not claim trusted built authority.
Promotion to retained implementation is deferred to a separate explicit build, tenant integration, and approval event.
